ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet avser en leverans till Calderys India Refractories Limited i Indien med produkten Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8 och nettovikt 25 200 kg, men fakturan är ställd till Haznedar Durer Refrakter Malzemeleri San. Ve Tic. A.S i Istanbul, Turkiet, med produkten Silica Patch HQ 26 UC och bruttovikt 575 kg. Varken mottagare, varubeskrivning eller kvantitet överensstämmer mellan dokumenten.
overall_assessment.summary: Dokumentparet uppvisar allvarliga avvikelser på flera kontrollpunkter. Certifikatets consignee (Calderys India Refractories Limited, India) kan inte identifieras i fakturan som är ställd till Haznedar Durer Refrakter i Istanbul. Varubeskrivningen i certifikatet (Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8) matchar inte fakturans vara (Silica Patch HQ 26 UC). Kvantiteten i certifikatet (NW 25 200 kg) motsäger fakturans vikter (NW 550 KGS, GW 575 KGS). Consignor och ursprungsland kan verifieras. Tre av fem kontrollpunkter ger MISMATCH.

certificate.readability: Good – text-based PDF with clear fields
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text extraction. Standard ICC Certificate of Origin format with bilingual EN/SE headings.

invoice.readability: Good – text-based PDF with clear fields
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text extraction. Standard proforma invoice from Calderys Nordic AB.

lc_check.lc_verification_steps: Searched certificate for LC/L/C/Letter of Credit/Documentary Credit references. The remarks field contains 'Material according to your order 2254546024' — this is an order reference, not an LC reference. No LC-related terms found. LC exception not activated.

occurrences_in_invoice_files.location: Footer block at bottom of page
occurrences_in_invoice_files.context: Company identification block with full address: Box 216, SE-263 23 Höganäs, VAT No. SE556012980001
Brand logo at top left of invoice (calderys with stylized symbol)

consignor_search_all_files.conclusion: Calderys Nordic AB is explicitly identified in the invoice footer block with full company details including VAT number and address. The Calderys brand also appears in the header logo.

criteria_checked.detail: Certificate does not contain an explicit invoice number. Remarks field contains order reference 2254546024. Invoice shows 'Your order no: 23CEX-0112' and 'Our order no: 2230003155'. Neither matches the certificate's order reference.
Certificate consignor 'Calderys Nordic AB' is explicitly identified in the invoice footer as 'Calderys Nordic AB, Sweden' with VAT No. SE556012980001.

Certificate consignee 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice. Invoice delivery/invoice address is 'Haznedar Durer Refrakter Malzemeleri San. Ve Tic. A.S' in Istanbul, Turkey.

pairing_validation.pairing_rationale: At least one criterion is met: consignor 'Calderys Nordic AB' is identifiable in the invoice. Pairing is valid, but consignee and goods discrepancies will be assessed in the checkpoint verification.

candidates.raw_text: Fidvi Tower, 6th floor, Opposite Saraf Chambers, Mount Road, 440001 Sadar Nagpur, India
candidates.normalized_text: fidvi tower, 6th floor, opposite saraf chambers, mount road, 440001 sadar nagpur, india

candidates.raw_text: HAZNEDAR DURER REFRAKTER MALZEMELERI SAN. VE TIC. A.S
candidates.normalized_text: haznedar durer refrakter malzemeleri san. ve tic. a.s

candidates.raw_text: Büyükcavuslu mah. Cerkezköy cad. NO: 585, TR-34594 SILIVRI ISTANBUL
candidates.normalized_text: büyükcavuslu mah. cerkezköy cad. no: 585, tr-34594 silivri istanbul

checkpoints.certificate_value: Calderys Nordic AB, Forshammarsgatan 2, 66232, Åmål, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Calderys Nordic AB, Sweden (footer block: Box 216, SE-263 23 Höganäs, VAT No. SE556012980001)

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in all invoice files before sequential testing.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate consignor 'Calderys Nordic AB' is explicitly identified in the invoice footer as 'Calderys Nordic AB, Sweden' with full address and VAT number SE556012980001. The brand 'calderys' also appears in the header logo. Name match is exact after normalization. Country: Sweden is explicit in both the footer text and the VAT prefix 'SE'.

rules_applied.rule_description: Corporate structure – consignor name found explicitly in invoice identification block
Land verification via indirect identifiers – VAT prefix SE confirms Sweden

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Calderys Nordic AB' in Sweden. Invoice footer explicitly states 'Calderys Nordic AB, Sweden' with address Box 216, SE-263 23 Höganäs and VAT No. SE556012980001. Name match is exact. Country Sweden confirmed both by explicit text and SE-prefix in VAT number. Although the invoice's Delivery address and Invoice address point to a Turkish company (Haznedar Durer Refrakter), the consignor verification is about the SENDER – and Calderys Nordic AB is the invoice issuer as confirmed in the footer. Rule 4.1.0 gives MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Consignor does not appear in invoice because the delivery/invoice address is a different company
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The consignor Calderys Nordic AB is the ISSUER of the invoice, explicitly identified in the footer with full legal details. The delivery address is the buyer/recipient, not the sender.

checkpoints.motivation: Calderys Nordic AB is explicitly identified as the invoice issuer in the footer block with full company name, address, and VAT number. Country Sweden is confirmed via explicit text and VAT SE-prefix. MATCH per rule 4.1.0.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Calderys India Refractories Limited, Fidvi Tower, 6th floor, Opposite Saraf Chambers, Mount Road, 440001 Sadar Nagpur, India
checkpoints.invoice_value: HAZNEDAR DURER REFRAKTER MALZEMELERI SAN. VE TIC. A.S, Büyükcavuslu mah. Cerkezköy cad. NO: 585, TR-34594 SILIVRI ISTANBUL (Invoice address: Same as Delivery address)

comparison_steps.description: Check primary invoicing field for consignee identification.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice address states 'Same as Delivery address', which points to HAZNEDAR DURER REFRAKTER MALZEMELERI SAN. VE TIC. A.S in Istanbul, Turkey. This is a completely different company from certificate consignee 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' in India.

comparison_steps.description: Check delivery address and other fields for consignee identification.
comparison_steps.detail: Delivery address is HAZNEDAR DURER REFRAKTER MALZEMELERI SAN. VE TIC. A.S in Turkey. Certificate consignee 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' does not appear anywhere in the invoice.

comparison_steps.step: Full text search for 'Calderys India Refractories' in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Search entire invoice text for certificate consignee name.
comparison_steps.detail: The string 'Calderys India Refractories' does not appear anywhere in the invoice text. The only 'Calderys' reference is to 'Calderys Nordic AB' (the consignor/issuer).

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate consignee appears in Ship-To field.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate consignee 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' does not appear in any field of the invoice. Ship-To exception cannot apply.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate consignee country is India. Invoice delivery/billing country is Turkey.

rules_applied.rule_description: Priority order for consignee identification – invoice-to field checked first
rules_applied.outcome: MISMATCH – Invoice address points to Haznedar Durer in Turkey

rules_applied.rule_description: Identification in invoice – searched all relevant fields
rules_applied.outcome: NOT_FOUND – Calderys India Refractories Limited not found

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' in Nagpur, India. The invoice is addressed to 'HAZNEDAR DURER REFRAKTER MALZEMELERI SAN. VE TIC. A.S' in Istanbul, Turkey (both delivery and invoice address). The name 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' does not appear anywhere in the invoice. The companies are in different countries (India vs Turkey) with completely different names. No normalization rule can bridge this gap. This is a clear MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Both companies contain 'Calderys' so they might be related
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.1.3.1 prohibits assuming corporate relationships. The certificate consignee is 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' and the invoice addressee is 'Haznedar Durer Refrakter Malzemeleri San. Ve Tic. A.S' – completely different legal entities in different countries. The word 'Calderys' appears in the invoice only as the ISSUER (Calderys Nordic AB), not as the ADDRESSEE.

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' (India) cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice. The invoice is addressed to 'Haznedar Durer Refrakter Malzemeleri San. Ve Tic. A.S' (Turkey). Different company, different country. MISMATCH per rules 4.2.1, 4.2.3, 4.2.4.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains article numbers to match against invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate does not contain an explicit article number. The goods description is 'CALDERYS INDIA / Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8'. No article/item number field is present.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation and compare.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate identity-bearing main designation: 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8'. Invoice product designation: 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC'. These are completely different product names. 'Svenska Mix 7 A60' cannot be identified in the invoice text. 'Silica Patch HQ 26 UC' cannot be identified in the certificate text. No normalization can reconcile these names.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 Priority 3 – Generic description with invoice reference
comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains generic description with invoice reference.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate contains a specific product name 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8', not a generic description. Additionally, no invoice number or invoice date is referenced in the certificate. The remarks contain order reference 2254546024 which does not match any invoice reference in the invoice.

rules_applied.rule_description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation
rules_applied.outcome: MISMATCH – 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8' ≠ 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC'

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate describes the goods as 'CALDERYS INDIA / Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8'. The invoice describes the goods as 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC' (item no. 1011513). These are entirely different product names with no overlapping text. 'Svenska Mix' is a refractory mix product; 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC' is a silica patching material. They share no common words in their product designations. Per rule 4.3.3, if the identity-bearing main designation cannot be identified in the invoice, the result is MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Both products might be refractory materials and thus equivalent
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.3.3 explicitly prohibits synonymy, semantic interpretation, and industry-based interpretation. The product names must match textually. 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8' and 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC' share no textual overlap.

checkpoints.motivation: The identity-bearing main designation 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8' in the certificate cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice. The invoice describes a completely different product 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC'. MISMATCH per rule 4.3.3.

checkpoints.invoice_value: NET WEIGHT 550 KGS / GROSS WEIGHT 575 KGS

comparison_steps.description: Determine the verification-basis quantity from the certificate.
comparison_steps.detail: Box 7 contains 'NW 25,200 kg'. This is the verifying quantity. The comma in '25,200' needs analysis: in Swedish context (certificate is from Sweden), comma is the decimal separator. However, 25.200 kg (25.2 kg) for a net weight seems very low. Alternatively, the comma could be a thousands separator giving 25200 kg. Given the Swedish notation convention and that the document uses Swedish formatting, '25,200 kg' most likely means 25 200 kg (twenty-five thousand two hundred kilograms) using comma as thousands separator. This interpretation is also consistent with typical industrial refractory shipment weights.

comparison_steps.description: Apply thousands separator normalization to certificate value.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate value '25,200 kg' normalized to 25200 kg. The comma serves as thousands separator (integer value, Swedish notation).

comparison_steps.description: Check if 25200 kg (NW) can be identified in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice states NET WEIGHT 550 KGS and GROSS WEIGHT 575 KGS. Neither 25200, 550, nor 575 match. 25200 kg ≠ 550 KGS. The difference is massive (25200 vs 550 = factor of ~45.8). No normalization or tolerance can bridge this gap.

comparison_steps.description: Check if unit conversion could resolve the discrepancy.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice quantity is 0.55 TONS in the item line. 0.55 TONS = 550 KG. Certificate NW is 25200 kg. 550 ≠ 25200. Conversion does not help.

rules_applied.outcome: NW 25200 kg identified as verification basis

rules_applied.rule_description: Thousands separator normalization – comma treated as thousands separator
Occurrence check – numeric value must be identifiable in invoice
Explicit contradiction – certificate NW 25200 kg vs invoice NW 550 KGS
rules_applied.outcome: MISMATCH – explicit contradiction in same weight category

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: 'NW 25,200 kg'. Normalized to NW 25200 kg (comma as thousands separator per 4.4.3.3 – integer value, Swedish document). Invoice: NET WEIGHT 550 KGS, GROSS WEIGHT 575 KGS. The certificate's NW of 25200 kg cannot be identified in the invoice. The invoice's NW is 550 KGS which is drastically different. This is an explicit contradiction per 4.4.3.1: both documents explicitly state a net weight value and they differ enormously (25200 vs 550). MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The comma in '25,200' is a decimal separator meaning 25.2 kg
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: While Swedish uses comma as decimal separator, 25.2 kg is an implausibly low net weight for an industrial refractory shipment with pallet packaging. The thousands-separator interpretation (25200 kg) is far more plausible and still doesn't match the invoice.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Perhaps the invoice is a partial shipment
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Even if this were a partial shipment, the verification is against the bifogade faktura. Certificate NW 25200 kg cannot be identified in invoice NW 550 KGS. Rule 4.4.1 requires the numeric value to be identifiable in the invoice.

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate states NW 25,200 kg (normalized: 25200 kg). Invoice states NET WEIGHT 550 KGS. These values constitute an explicit contradiction for the same weight category (Net Weight). The value 25200 cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice. MISMATCH per rules 4.4.1 and 4.4.3.1.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin statement in invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: Sweden' below the item table.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'EU Sweden'. Check if Sweden + EU can be verified against invoice's 'Sweden'.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate origin: 'EU Sweden'. Sweden is an EU member state. Per rule 4.5.4.2.1, when certificate states 'EU [country]' (EU combined with ONE specific EU member state) and invoice contains an explicit origin statement confirming Sweden, this constitutes a match. The invoice states 'Country of Origin: Sweden' which is a dedicated origin field. Sweden is an EU member state, so 'EU Sweden' in the certificate is fully verified by 'Sweden' in the invoice.

rules_applied.rule_description: Identification of origin in invoice – explicit 'Country of Origin: Sweden' found
EU member state normalization – Sweden is EU member
EU combined with one specific member state – invoice confirms Sweden

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states 'EU Sweden'. Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Sweden is an EU member state. Per rule 4.5.4.2, the exchange between EU and a specific member state is valid in both directions. Per 4.5.4.2.1, when the certificate states 'EU [one specific EU member state]' and the invoice's dedicated origin field confirms that country, this is MATCH. No non-EU origins appear in the invoice. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Certificate origin 'EU Sweden' is verified against invoice's explicit 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Sweden is an EU member state. Per rules 4.5.4.2 and 4.5.4.2.1, this constitutes full verification. MATCH.

evidence.location: Footer - VAT / EU VAT no. field

evidence.raw_text: HAZNEDAR DURER REFRAKTER MALZEMELERI SAN. VE TIC. A.S, Büyükcavuslu mah. Cerkezköy cad. NO: 585, TR-34594 SILIVRI ISTANBUL

rule_evaluation_log.detail: No LC reference found in certificate. Order reference 2254546024 does not constitute an LC reference.
Document A identified as certificate (ICC Certificate of Origin). Document B identified as invoice (Proforma Invoice from Calderys Nordic AB). Standard pairing applied.
Criterion 2 met: Consignor 'Calderys Nordic AB' identified in invoice footer. Pairing is valid.
Calderys Nordic AB found explicitly in invoice footer with full legal details.
Invoice address = Delivery address = Haznedar Durer Refrakter (Turkey). Certificate consignee Calderys India Refractories Limited (India) not found.
Certificate consignee does not appear in any field of the invoice.
Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8 cannot be identified in invoice. Invoice describes SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC.
NW 25200 kg not identifiable in invoice. Invoice NW 550 KGS. Explicit contradiction.
Even with conversion, values don't match (25200 kg ≠ 550 kg).

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: EU combined with one specific member state
rule_evaluation_log.detail: Certificate 'EU Sweden' verified against invoice 'Country of Origin: Sweden'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The documents might refer to the same transaction despite the discrepancies
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Three critical discrepancies exist: (1) Different consignee – Calderys India Refractories Limited (India) vs Haznedar Durer Refrakter (Turkey), (2) Different goods – Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8 vs SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC, (3) Vastly different weights – NW 25200 kg vs NW 550 KGS. The order references also don't match (certificate: 2254546024, invoice: 23CEX-0112 / 2230003155). These are clearly documents from different transactions.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: This should be MANUAL_REVIEW rather than NOT_IDENTICAL since the consignor and origin match
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 6.1, NOT_IDENTICAL is required when at least one applicable checkpoint gives MISMATCH. Three checkpoints give MISMATCH (consignee, goods, quantity). Per the consistency requirement in Step 6, comparison_result MUST be NOT_IDENTICAL.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Calderys Nordic AB, Forshammarsgatan 2, 66232, Åmål, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Calderys Nordic AB, Sweden (footer: Box 216, SE-263 23 Höganäs, VAT No. SE556012980001)
sections.rule_applied: Consignor identified in invoice via corporate structure rule (4.1.0). Name match exact. Country Sweden confirmed via explicit text and VAT SE-prefix.
sections.motivation: Calderys Nordic AB is explicitly named as the invoice issuer in the footer block with VAT number SE556012980001. Country Sweden verified. MATCH.

sections.certificate_value: Calderys India Refractories Limited, Fidvi Tower, 6th floor, Opposite Saraf Chambers, Mount Road, 440001 Sadar Nagpur, India
sections.invoice_value: HAZNEDAR DURER REFRAKTER MALZEMELERI SAN. VE TIC. A.S, Büyükcavuslu mah. Cerkezköy cad. NO: 585, TR-34594 SILIVRI ISTANBUL
sections.rule_applied: Consignee name not found anywhere in invoice. Different company and different country.
sections.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Calderys India Refractories Limited' (India) cannot be identified in the invoice. Invoice is addressed to 'Haznedar Durer Refrakter Malzemeleri San. Ve Tic. A.S' (Turkey). Completely different company in a different country. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Identity-bearing main designation 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8' cannot be identified in invoice product 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC'. Completely different products.
sections.motivation: Certificate describes 'Svenska Mix 7 A60 0,8'. Invoice describes 'SILICA PATCH HQ 26 UC'. No textual overlap in product designation. MISMATCH.

sections.invoice_value: NET WEIGHT 550 KGS / GROSS WEIGHT 575 KGS
sections.rule_applied: Certificate NW 25200 kg (thousands separator normalized) cannot be identified in invoice. Invoice NW is 550 KGS. Explicit contradiction in same weight category.
sections.motivation: Certificate NW 25200 kg vs invoice NW 550 KGS. Values differ by factor ~45.8. Explicit contradiction. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Certificate 'EU Sweden' verified against invoice 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Sweden is an EU member state. EU/member state equivalence applied.
sections.motivation: Certificate origin 'EU Sweden' fully verified by invoice's explicit 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. MATCH.

human_readable_report.overall_result: NOT_IDENTICAL – Three of five checkpoints result in MISMATCH (consignee, goods description, quantity). The certificate and invoice appear to relate to different transactions entirely.

output_quality_checks.consistency_detail: Three checkpoints (consignee, goods, quantity) have status MISMATCH → comparison_result is NOT_IDENTICAL. No motivations contradict their respective statuses. All MATCH motivations support MATCH conclusions. All MISMATCH motivations support MISMATCH conclusions.
